pattern: The XOR of two independent subset-XORs ranges over the whole linear span of the values, so the answer is the maximum element of the binary linear basis.
hints: Each chosen subsequence's XOR can be any value reachable by XOR-ing some elements; the two choices are independent.
If a value v is reachable as a subset XOR, so is any combination, and XOR-ing two reachable values stays inside the same set; that set is closed under XOR.
Build a binary linear basis (Gaussian elimination over GF(2)) of the numbers, then greedily combine basis vectors from the highest bit down to maximize the result.

edge_inputs.description: two values whose XOR is their disjoint-bit sum
minimum length with a zero present
all equal values span only one basis vector
values whose XOR cancels to a smaller span
powers of two combine to all-ones

stress.description: many values over a wide range so the basis fills several bit positions
